events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: A/C service, sealed with stop-leak before service (TRD). Work rough-complete. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. We need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Condition: sealed with stop-leak before service. Stop-leak is generally not recommended by OEM, can contaminate refrigerant, damage compressor, void warranty, violate EPA 609? EPA 609 prohibits adding refrigerant to leaking system without repair, and stop-leak may be considered unacceptable. Also TRD likely "temporary repair device"? The system was sealed with stop-leak before service. This is non-compliant, installed-defective. Need provide value? Maybe value 0 because no acceptable stop-leak amount; or value representing stop-leak present. The task says estimate or interpret measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. We can set value 0 (no OEM-approved stop-leak additive concentration; any stop-leak is out of spec). Confidence high. Risk high/critical? Stop-leak can cause compressor failure, contamination, safety. I'd choose high. Decision fail. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 60. Findings: system sealed with stop-leak additive prior to service; stop-leak not approved by OEM; contaminates refrigerant and may damage compressor/expansion valve; violates proper leak repair practice under EPA 609. Actions: recover refrigerant, flush system per OEM, repair leak with approved methods, replace contaminated components/drier, evacuate and recharge to spec, verify leak-free. Rationale. References: EPA 609, OEM A/C service specs. Need include all fields. sound_source, event_time, rate, order, workable: not audio/video, use defaults empty string, 0, empty list. order maybe empty list. workable empty list. Ensure valid JSON.

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],

  "findings": [
    "A/C system was sealed with stop-leak additive before service",
    "Stop-leak is not an OEM-approved repair method and contaminates the refrigerant circuit",
    "Additive residue can damage compressor, expansion valve, and drier",
    "Sealing a leak without proper repair violates EPA 609 leak-repair requirements"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Recover refrigerant in accordance with EPA 609",
    "Inspect and flush the A/C system per OEM procedure",
    "Replace contaminated components such as drier/accumulator and any damaged compressor parts",
    "Repair the leak using approved brazing, O-ring, or component replacement methods",
    "Evacuate, recharge, and leak-test to OEM specifications"
  ],
  "rationale": "Any stop-leak additive in the refrigerant circuit is out of tolerance because OEM A/C specifications do not approve sealants as a repair, the additive can contaminate oil and damage compressor/expansion devices, and EPA 609 requires proper leak repair rather than simply sealing a leaking system.",
  "references": ["EPA 609", "OEM A/C service specifications"]
}
